input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows the calculated values of TS voltage over time. For Tractive System Measuring Points (TSMP), a 10 kOhm resistor could be used to limit the current. Based on this image and rule EV.5.8.4 in the rule document, can we use the resistor for TSMP? First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with ‘Explanation:’). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with ‘Answer:’) that summarizes your response.

rag.search_query: EV.5.8.4 Tractive System Measuring Points resistor current limit
retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.5.8.4
            TITLE: each tsmp must be secured with a current limiting resistor.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.8.4: Each TSMP must be secured with a current limiting resistor.
EV.5.8.4 Each TSMP must be secured with a current limiting resistor.   
a. The resistor must be sized per the following: 
Maximum TS Voltage (Vmax)  Resistor Value  
Vmax <= 200 V DC   5 kOhm 
200 V DC < Vmax <= 400 V DC  10 kOhm 
400 V DC < Vmax <= 600 V DC  15 kOhm 
b. Resistor continuous power rating must be more than the power dissipated across the 
TSMPs if they are shorted together  
c. Direct measurement of the value of the resistor must be possible during Electrical 
Technical Inspection.

final_answer:
Explanation: Based on Rule EV.5.8.4, a 10 kOhm resistor is appropriate only if the maximum calculated TS voltage ($V_{max}$) falls within the range of $200 \text{ V DC} < V_{max} \le 400 \text{ V DC}$. Assuming your analysis confirms that $V_{max}$ meets this specific criteria, then requirement (a) is satisfied. However, please note that using the resistor does not satisfy the entire rule. You must also ensure that: b) The continuous power rating of the chosen resistor is greater than the maximum power dissipated if all TSMPs were shorted together, and c) That the value of the resistor can be directly measured during the Electrical Technical Inspection. If these conditions are met in addition to the voltage range check, then the 10 kOhm resistor is acceptable for sizing purposes.

Answer: Yes
